ddls:
CREATE TABLE "states" (
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each state */,
  "state_name" TEXT /* Name of the state */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographic region of the state */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the state */,
  "area" REAL /* Area of the state in square kilometers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shape_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the shape type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the shape type (e.g., Circle, Triangle) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the shape type */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the shape (e.g., simple, complex) */,
  "attributes" TEXT /* Additional attributes in JSON format */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shape_occurrences" (
  "occurrence_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each occurrence record */,
  "shape_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the shape entry */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of shape */,
  "count" INTEGER /* Count of occurrences of the shape type */,
  "location_description" TEXT /* Description of the location where the shape was observed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("occurrence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shape_occurrences_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES shape_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shape_changes" (
  "change_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change record */,
  "shape_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the shape entry */,
  "changed_on" TEXT /* Date when the change was recorded */,
  "previous_count" INTEGER /* Previous count of the shape */,
  "current_count" INTEGER /* Current count of the shape */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who recorded the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("change_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shape_changes_changed_by FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "shape_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shape being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the shape was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shape_metadata" (
  "metadata_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metadata entry */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the shape type */,
  "metadata_key" TEXT /* Key of the metadata attribute */,
  "metadata_value" TEXT /* Value of the metadata attribute */,
  "metadata_type" TEXT /* Type of metadata (e.g., numeric, textual) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metadata_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shape_metadata_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES shape_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shape_visualizations" (
  "visualization_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each visualization */,
  "shape_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the shape entry */,
  "image_path" TEXT /* File path to the visualization image */,
  "created_on" TEXT /* Date when the visualization was created */,
  "visualization_type" TEXT /* Type of visualization (e.g., 2D, 3D) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("visualization_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shape_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_title" TEXT /* Title of the report */,
  "report_content" TEXT /* Content or summary of the report */,
  "created_on" TEXT /* Date when the report was created */,
  "generated_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who generated the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shape_reports_generated_by FOREIGN KEY ("generated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
